timeline_html:
Summary. Eryomi has noticed shedding that comes in waves for more than a year, with recent baby hairs suggesting her follicles are becoming active again. Her timeline links closely to long-standing hormonal changes around perimenopause and ongoing high stress from life demands.

Your hair-loss triggers.

  • Perimenopause (ongoing; started date not provided)
  • Chronic stress (ongoing; started Mar 2020)
  • Pregnancy-related (resolved; ended Feb 2020)

Timeline forecast. The pregnancy-related shedding likely peaked around Apr–May 2020 and stabilized by late 2020. Since then, hormonal transition and continuous stress have maintained mild, wave-like shedding. Based on typical lag patterns, stabilization for past acute loss would have occurred by 2020‑11, with regrowth beginning in early 2021—but the ongoing stress and perimenopause may extend these windows slightly. Dominant driver: Chronic stress since Mar 2020 aligns with her fluctuating pattern and remains active today, reinforcing the ongoing nature of her shedding.

Where you are now. You’re in a stabilization phase—new growth has begun, even if shedding still flares at times. With continued stress support and hormonal balance, the regrowth phase should become more noticeable soon. 🌱
